Lacking Sanggunian Bayan (SB) Ordinance
In relation with the above augmentation of the savings to a new project, Purchase of trucks for road construction and maintenance amounting to ₱4,000,000.00, a supplemental Annual Investment Program (AIP) was approved on June 14, 2024. However, this was not supported with SB Ordinance adopting and approving the said supplemental AIP.
Pursuant to the guidelines above, the SB ordinance is necessary to operationalize the fund augmentation for the additional project under the 20 per cent DF adopted by Municipal Development Council. Otherwise, any obligation/disbursement charged from the said allotment would be considered irregular.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC-Designate, and Municipal Budget Officer to submit the SB Ordinance adopting and approving the supplemental AIP of the additional project under the 20 per cent DF.
The Management submitted the copy of MDC 24-004 approved on October 14, 2024 rectifying MDC Resolution No. 24-001 in which the appropriation deficit amounting to ₱98,000.00 was instead sourced from the PPA, Construction/Development of Municipal Tourism Facilities and Structures, to resolve the first deficiency. Also, SB Resolution Nos. 2025-41 and 2025-40 both approved March 10, 2025 to ratify the adoption of Supplemental Annual Investment Plan 2024 incorporating the purchase of trucks for road construction and maintenance amounting to ₱4,000,000.00 and adoption of MDC Resolution No. 2024-001.
Although the appropriation deficit was rectified later, the SB Resolution No. 2025-40 adopted the MDC Resolution in which the error was present. Thereby, the Audit Team maintains the position to require the Management to submit SB Resolution that aligns with the MDC Resolution with the correct budget appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Executive Committee produce the legal basis of its creation and submit the Minutes of Meetings where the concerned Accountable Officer was authorized to collect/solicit for special projects of the Municipality.  Submit the covering disbursement vouchers and supporting receipts to support disbursements totaling P1,953,730.00, otherwise the same will be disallowed in audit.

observations.recommendation:
In accordance with Section 7.4 of the IRR of 2016, we recommend that the BAC Secretariat prepare an amended APP incorporating the information required in Section 7.3.4.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor send the BAC Chairperson, Members of the BAC and Secretariat including the members of the Technical Working Group to training/s on procurement to professionalize its procurement officials in order to promote and attain good governance by adhering to the principles of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in its procurement process.
We recommend that Management particularly, the BAC submit the documents identified as lacking to prevent the disallowance of the transaction in audit.

We recommend that the BAC Secretariat update the Municipality’s APP by including items that the agency wants to procure but are not initially consolidated therein and have it approved by the Mayor as head of the procuring entity before buying goods and materials not included in the APP. Logically, only those items that have the corresponding funds/appropriations shall be consolidated into the Supplemental APP.

Moreover, submit a valid explanation on why the procurement of materials for the construction of a new POATAB Project (Phase 3-A) was not included in the APP to prevent disallowance in audit.

We recommend that Management submit a valid written explanation as to why the Local Councils for the Protection of Children was not created and funds not appropriated for the implementation of its programs and projects as required under the above provisions. Submit a copy of a Sangguniang Bayan Resolution or Executive Order creating the LCPC as well as its AWFP prioritized according to needs, for integration in the local development plan pursuant to DILG Memo Circular No. 2012-120.

Henceforth, strictly observe the above rules and regulations to prevent administrative sanctions that maybe imposed thereon.

We recommend that Management send Final Demand Letters to these debtors and to exhaust all possible remedies to enforce settlement thereof.

For those separated from the government service due to death and resignations, the Municipality may request for a write-off of their accounts duly supported with Demand Letters and proof of separation/death and other valid documents supporting the efforts made by Management.

Henceforth, Management should conduct regular monitoring and periodic analysis of unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers to ensure that these are collected when these become due and demandable and that these are liquidated within the prescribed period pursuant to existing COA rules and regulations.

We recommend that Management, particularly the BAC, submit the documents mentioned under Sections 8.5.1 and 24.1 of R.A 9184 as well as a valid written explanation, on why there was no public bidding conducted in the procurement of Consultancy Services to prevent the disallowance of the transactions in audit.

Henceforth, all procurement, including the hiring of services, should be subjected to public bidding if the cost exceeds the threshold of P100,000.00 and should be duly supported with the documents mentioned under Sections 8.5.1 and 24.1 of R.A 9184.  In the same manner, secure the written acquiescence of the Solicitor General and concurrence of the COA to avoid disallowance of the payments for Legal Consultancy amounting to P140,000.00. Likewise, stop the hiring of Consulting Services which are not highly technical in nature.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry voucher (copy-furnished the COA) reclassifying the capital expenditures for POATAB amounting to P297,055.45 under account 1-07-03-040 (Water Supply Systems) and the remaining balance of medicines and garbage bins on hand as of December 31, 2017 under the corresponding ‘Inventory” accounts.

Henceforth, major repairs which could enhance the life of a Property, Plant and Equipment should be capitalized. Likewise, supplies, materials and medicines should be taken up as “Inventories” upon purchase and charged to Expense account only upon actual issuance to end-users based on the Monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued/ Inventory Custodian Slip.

We recommend that the Management cause the submission of the duly filled-up documents enumerated above and a valid explanation by the BAC on the deficiencies noted and on why the said procurements were split and were not subjected to public bidding but were made thru Negotiated Procurement, to avoid disallowance in audit. Management should prove the existence of conditions for emergency purchase.

Likewise, instruct the BAC to strictly follow the provisions of RA 9184 by subjecting procurements to public bidding especially for thresholds of One Hundred Thousand (P100,000.00) and above.

We recommend that Management, specifically the Municipal Engineer, submit a Certification that the POATAB Water System Phase II and 3-A have been fully inspected, completed and accepted duly approved by the Municipal Mayor and supported with proper documents. Likewise, submit a revised/correct Quarterly Project Status Report for the year 2017 to ascertain the date of completion of the POATAB Water System Phase II and 3-A.

Reiteration for the submission of the documents such as the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing the former mayor to enter into a contract with the contractor, Certificate of Turn-over and Completion by the Contractor and Inspection and Acceptance by Management in connection with the implementation of the Phase II project is hereby reiterated for inspection by our COA Inspector.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and other Municipal Officials concerned strictly observe the proper charging of the expenditures against the 20% Development Fund (DF) pursuant to DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

Henceforth, stop the practice of charging the project cost of the repairs and renovations of administrative buildings and the procurement of office furniture, fixtures and equipment out of the 20% DF.  Incurrence of the same nature may be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality implemented 100% of the COA`s recommendation starting February 2017 and onwards.

observations.reason: Management did not submit the consultancy contracts for the three (3) consultants of the Municipality and other documents required to support the validity of the payments for consultancy fees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management stop the practice of procuring goods and services thru cash advances or reimbursements but procurement should be based on properly conducted canvass and by paying directly to suppliers such that corresponding taxes could be deducted therefrom and purchases would be properly supported with Official Receipts (ORs) and not just

 Reimbursement Expense Receipts (RERs) to ascertain the validity and propriety of disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Procurement thru cash advance or reimbursement is more convenient.
observations.reason: The post-audit of transactions disclosed that Management still resorted to cash advances/reimbursements in the procurement of goods and services.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the municipal treasurer exercise utmost diligence in the performance of her disbursing function. She is reminded not to issue checks if the DV lacked proper signatures and has no complete supporting documents. 

Further, Management should see to it that all claims against the municipal funds are covered with duly approved DVs and complete documentation to ascertain that disbursements were actually incurred  and delivery of items procured have been ensured. Submit the lacking documents to support disbursements totalling P1,728,003.32,otherwise the same will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed that DVs were already properly signed and approved before payments are being made.
observations.reason: Review and post-audit of transactions revealed that some of the mentioned disbursement vouchers (DVs)were still not signed and approved by concerned Municipal officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management should observe the rules and regulations governing the use of government vehicles and effect the mark “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY”, three (3) inches in size and the corresponding name of the agency on each side of the vehicle to prevent any unnecessary and unofficial use of government motor vehicles (COA Circular No. 75-6).
observations.managementAction: All vehicles under the name of the Municipality was not marked “For Official Use Only.”
observations.reason: Inspection of the Motor vehicles of the Municipality revealed that it was not marked “For Official Use Only.”

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accountant enforce immediate settlement of all outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2013 by sending demand letters to each accountable officer. However, if these accountable officers fail to comply, the Local Chief Executive should advise in writing the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to suspend the salaries of the officials  and employees to comply settlement of their outstanding accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management still granted cash advances to its employees especially the DSWD Officer for supplemental feeding and for socio-cultural activities.
observations.reason: Management still granted additional cash advances to its officers and employees even if previous ones were not liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict compliance with Section 32 and 34of the Manual on the NGAS for Local Government Units. The Municipal Treasurer should deposit intact collections as well as all collections turned over to her by the collectors/tellers with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day to prevent possible loss or misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The designated Municipal Treasurer failed to monitor remittances of sub-collectors, thus accumulating collections on hand.
observations.reason: Cash examination conducted by the Cebu Provincial Government showed that the sub-collectors did not remit all collections to the acting Municipal Treasurer on time and were delayed by up to more than ten (10) months, thus causing delay in the deposits thereof.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommend that Management intensify its tax collection drive by issuing regularly billings/ notice to taxpayers and making personal follow-ups constantly on delinquent ones. The LGU should avail of available legal remedies either by

administrative action through levy on real property or judicial actions as either by administrative action through levy on real property or judicial actions as authorized under Section 256,257 and 258 of the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: Management failed to monitor collections of Real Property Taxes (RPT) because of lack of personnel.
observations.reason: Collections for RPT was still minimal.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommend that Management stop immediately granting of the Communication Allowance to both individuals and for both Dr.Cang and Mr.Alcaria to refund the full amount of P6,000 each for the allowance given in 2012. Henceforth, any allowances and benefits given to all job-order employees and consultants shall be disallowed automatically.
observations.managementAction: Management failed to collect the refund of communication allowance granted to Dr.Cang and Alcaria but they planned to send collection letters to them.
observations.reason: No refund was noted from the concerned personnel for claims of Communication Allowance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:
1. Deliveries of medical supplies must be accepted first by the General Service Office (GSO) Municipal Officer, as the case may be, before inspection.
2. Require the Rural Health Unit (RHU) to control issuance of medicines by maintaining a logbook with complete data.
3. Create an inventory committee to conduct a semi-annual physical inventory of medicines and office supplies, and require the RHU Officer to submit a Monthly Report of Medicines Issued to the accountant for recording of issuances.
observations.managementAction: Purchase of medical supplies and equipment was still immediately issued to the RHU and did not pass through the GSO and the same has not created nor conducted inventory of said supplies, thus no Inventory Report was prepared.
observations.reason: The RHU was required to submit an Inventory Report to support purchases and issuances of medicines and supplies but failed to produce the same.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the employee in charge of processing the repair and maintenance of the agency’s vehicles and other equipment should require the submission or attachment of the Pre-repair Inspection Report, stating the extent of repair work tobe done on the vehicle/ equipment for repair, to guide the Property inspector in the post-repair inspection. The Post-repair Inspection Report must be prepared by the Inspector stating whether the work specified/ required in the Pre-Inspection Report was actually

undertaken or not. These documents shall be attached as supporting documents to the DV.

Moreover, a record of repair of vehicles, and other equipment must be kept by the agency indicating there in the type of repair undertaken, the date when it was made, and other pertinent data for the information and guidance of the COA, the agency and others concerned.
observations.managementAction: Repairs of vehicles and other equipment were still not supported with pre-repair Inspection Report. Although sometimes an Inspection and Acceptance Report were attached, it did not contain the date of inspection.
observations.reason:
Repairs of vehicles and other equipment were still not supported with pre-repair Inspection Report, although sometimes an Inspection and Acceptance Report was attached, it did not contain the date of 

inspection. Also, record of repairs of the said vehicles still was not maintained by the Accounting Unit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive must ensure that lots owned by the LGU be registered under the Torrens System in order to protect the interest of the municipal government.
observations.managementAction: Management justified that processing of said documents is a tedious process.
observations.reason: Verification of the records in the Assessor’s Office showed that the Lots owned by the Municipality have not been issued Transfer Certificate of Title but only Tax Declarations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10,1997 and Section 49 (b) and (i) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government  Units, Volume I. The Disbursing Officer should only be allowed to draw cash advances for payroll fund equal to the net amount of the duly certified and approved payrolls.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer justified that the Accounting unit was in-charge of the preparation of the disbursement voucher or payroll and she will just issue checks in her name based on the documents presented to her. Management further justified that the salary of the Job Order (J.O) personnel was based on estimates because they were paid together with the regular personnel.
observations.reason: The post-audit of paid payrolls as against cash advances made still showed that the previous Municipal Treasurer (MT) drew cash advances more than the liquidations made.

observations.recommendation: The Municipality should create a monitoring committee to determine whether all financial assistance extended to different barangays were really implemented based in their purposes. No additional financial assistance should be made unless the previous financial assistance had been liquidated.
observations.managementAction:
The Agency is in the process of creating a team to monitor the financial assistance.
Management cited lack of personnel as hindrance to the implementation of the audit recommendation.
observations.reason:
The Municipality still failed to monitor the Financial Assistance granted to the 

different barangays, thus implementation thereof are doubtful.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and other Municipal Officials concerned strictly observe theproper charging of the expenditures against the 20% Development Fund (DF) pursuant to DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
Henceforth, stop the practice of charging the project cost of the repairs and renovations of administrative buildings and the procurement of office furniture, fixtures and equipment out of the 20% DF.  Incurrence of the same nature may be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that Management submit the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing the then Municipal Mayor to enter into a contract with Philkonstrak Development Corporation. Also require the Municipal Engineer  and the Contractor to submit a written explanation on why the said project have not been completed during the scheduled completion date, otherwise, liquidated damages should be imposed thereon.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer clarify the project status of the Construction of the Philippine National Police (PNP) Building Phase II, otherwise, the construction materials and labor incurred after its reported accomplishments will be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that the former In-Charge of Office, Municipal Treasurer’s Office and the Officials who approved the payment thereof refund the amount of P68,703.75 representing payment of meals incurred for election-related expenses which violated the above provisions of law.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer explain in writing on the delays incurred in the implementation of Projects and to submit Extra Work or Variation Orders for projects that have been extended. He must strictly observe the number of days the project has to be implemented as contained in the Program of Work such that intended beneficiaries could immediately
avail of the facilities/services offered by the Municipality and to prevent issuance of disallowance/s.

We recommend that the former In-Charge of Office(ICO), Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) submit her Final Report of Accountability and the supporting Report of Collections and Deposits duly supported with duplicate Official Receipts and validated deposit slips for verification purposes. Exert efforts such that unreported collections be immediately deposited without further delay or she should be held accountable therefore for failure to monitor her sub-collectors.
Likewise, require the ICO- Municipal Accountant to record in the books the unreported collections amounting to P933,703.28 so as not to understate income during the year. Instruct the Municipal Mayor to monitor settlement thereof, otherwise, he maybe jointly and severally liable therefor.
Henceforth, require proper turn-over of accountabilities between incoming and outgoing officers duly signed by both parties duly witnessed by the COA. Any undeposited collections or unliquidated cash advance of the outgoing officer should be turned-over to the incoming officer for deposit and receipt of any excess amount.

We recommend that Management submit the Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and the concerned Peoples/Non-Government Organizations for evaluation purposes as well as its Monitoring Report on the projects undertaken by the latter. In addition, also submit the Work and Financial Plan, Financial Status, Fund Utilization Report and other lacking documents as mentioned in the preceding paragraphs.
Thus, instruct Management to strictly comply with the provisions of the above-mentioned Circular prior to the release of funds.
We also require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the account erroneously made.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) procure goods from an accredited supplier which sells items at a lower cost. Further the BAC should see to it that a job which could be performed by the Municipality’s work force is availed of to save on project cost.
Henceforth, reference to specific brand name in the procurement of goods should be prohibited pursuant to Section 18 of R.A 9184.

We recommend that Management strictly adhere to the General Guidelines in the granting and utilization of cash advances.
Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that granting of cash advance must be given to appointive/ permanent employees only. Elective officials may be granted a cash advance for their official travelling expenses only pursuant to the above provision.
On the other hand,	stop the practice of transferring cash advances from one officer to another especially to officers who are not bonded, not a regular plantilla personnel and who has no proper designation as disbursing officer to protect the interest of the government.

observations.managementAction: Management has already stopped the granting of cash advances of the same nature to the ICO Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: The ICO Municipal Treasurer has not been granted cash advances of the same nature except for payroll purposes which have been fully liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict compliance with Section 42 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume 2, so that pinpointing of accountability by end-users is facilitated.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office (GSO) has already prepared AREs for equipment which were updated from time to time.
observations.reason: In the inspection and validation of Properties of the Municipality, we have noted that the acting Property Officer has maintained an ARE for every Property issued to end-users.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Disaster Risk-Reduction Management Officer-Designate justify in writing why it did not conduct public bidding or canvass in the purchase of Local Disaster Risk -Reduction Management equipment and instead, were procured thru cash advances by him and thru Direct Contracting. Submit the liquidation documents to ascertain the validity and propriety of the purchase.
In addition, instruct the Officer concerned to prohibit himself from acting as Procuring Officer and to secure an Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment, otherwise, surrender the items to the Property Officer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Disaster Risk-Reduction Officer (MDRRMO) has stopped the procurement of LDRRM equipment thru cash advance.  In addition, he has submitted his justification letter in this regard.
observations.reason: The MDRRMO has submitted his justification on this matter, however, per evaluation, his reason was not valid, thus he was issued Notices of Suspension.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the acting Municipal Treasurer submit the disbursement vouchers totalling P611,302.37 so that these could be recorded in the books, otherwise, the same may be disallowed in audit. Further, advise the Accountant to record/adjust the reconciling items contained in the Bank Reconciliation Statements showing a net overstatement of P 118,935.61 to come up with correct Cash In Bank account balances.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer submitted some of the disbursement vouchers but


some has yet to be located.
observations.reason:
The lacking disbursement vouchers have not been completely 

submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Chairman, Bids and Awards Committee submit the above-mentioned documents/justifications to facilitate the audit in the procurement of one (1) unit Dump Truck, otherwise the same maybe disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management has yet to locate the documents supporting the disbursements.
observations.reason: Management still did not submit the required Contract Agreement, CAF and other necessary documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the concerned Municipal drivers accomplish properly pre-numbered DTT indicating the important data mentioned above. Purchase of fuel on account especially by each driver should be supported with duly approved RIV/PR/PO and original Charge Invoice every time a purchase is made.  The quantity purchased

should be recorded in the DTT. Fuel usage and balances should be indicated in the DTT. 

At the end of each month, submit the required Monthly Report of Official Travels and Fuel Consumption Report per vehicle for verification purposes.
observations.managementAction:
Driver’s Trip Tickets (DTTs) were already pre-numbered.
The ending balance of fuel could not be reflected because the odometer is not functioning. On the other hand, the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption has yet to be prepared.
observations.reason:
The post-audit of DTTs showed that it was already pre-numbered and procurements of fuel were recorded in the DTTs but were directly issued, thus no ending balance could be reflected. The Monthly Report of Official Travels and fuel Consumption Report have not been submitted to our end nor attached to the

disbursements for gasoline.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation to create an Inventory Committee, with a COA representative serving as witness, to immediately conduct actual physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE in order to determine its existence and to render a report thereon, a copy of which must be submitted to the Auditor in compliance with Section 124 Volume 1 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS). The Physical Inventory Report, also known as RPCPPE, should be reconciled with accounting records. Likewise we recommend for the maintenance of PPE ledger cards by the Accounting Section and stock/property ledger cards by the Property Officer or GSO-designate.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory Committee was already created headed by the GSO/Property Officer who conducted inventory of PPEs purchased during his time (excluding PY purchases which was not supported with Inventory report and Acknowledg-   ment for Property).
observations.reason: The newly-created Inventory Committee had conducted partial inventory of PPEs as it did not included procured items prior to their designation as such due to unavailability of records like the previous copy of Inventory Report and ARE, thus reconciliation of records with the Accounting Unit is not possible.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management should notify the public through signboards of their projects at the project location and direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Commission on Audit through the Audit Team Leader (ATL) the list of ongoing projects/programs/activities and

those that are to be implemented during the year and adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: Management had already installed Signboards in the premises where Infrastructures were undertaken by the Municipality.
observations.reason:
Inspection/validation conducted disclosed that not all on-going Infrastructure Projects were installed notifications/signboards.
The projects undertaken for the Rehabilitation/    renovation of the Municipal Building and the

 PNP Office have not been installed signboards informing the public of the status and costs incurred thereon as well as the construction of POATAB water system.
